notes.eos_confound: 279/4000 items rejected by EOS filter (min_gen_tokens=8). Only items generating >= 8 tokens nocontext are included.
notes.step10_imbalance: At step=10, only 16/44 PARAM items still generating (vs 38 CTX_DEP). AUROC=0.9056 computed on balanced n=16/class. PARAM survivors are verbose-answer items.
notes.finding: MONOTONE_RISE: For EOS-filtered verbose items, Fisher+PCA64 AUROC rises monotonically from 0.639 (step-0) to 0.906 (step-10). Contradicts PEAK_AT_1 hypothesis.
notes.two_populations: Short-answer PARAM items: step-0 signal then EOS at step 2-4 (not captured here — rejected by EOS filter). Verbose items: signal grows through generation. The bilateral oracle 'step-1 is privileged' claim was based on comparison to prefill (step-0 = last prompt token in ESM v33), not within-generation comparison.
